old_claim.note: Pharmacopoeia provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The specific Pliny encyclopedia is no longer modeled as depending on the separate broad pharmacopoeia category, which duplicated its medicinal-compilation meaning.

source_shape.source_locator: Stanford describes Natural History as a 77 CE, 37-book compilation of ancient Mediterranean knowledge for craftsmen and farmers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The reviewed record scopes the node to Pliny's identifiable 77 CE encyclopedia rather than to a generic pharmaceutical workflow.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. The specific Pliny encyclopedia is no longer modeled as depending on the separate broad pharmacopoeia category, which duplicated its medicinal-compilation meaning.

ontology_before: pharmacopoeia_compilation treated pharmacopoeia as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: The specific Pliny encyclopedia is no longer modeled as depending on the separate broad pharmacopoeia category, which duplicated its medicinal-compilation meaning.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pharmacopoeia_compilation no longer has pharmacopoeia as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes pharmacopoeia as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped pharmacopoeia_compilation claim.
short_reason: The specific Pliny encyclopedia is no longer modeled as depending on the separate broad pharmacopoeia category, which duplicated its medicinal-compilation meaning.
